sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, presented with a history of recurrent kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
She reported that the current stone had passed about three days ago and she was no longer experiencing pain or blood in her urine.
She has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes, and is currently taking norvasc 2.5mg daily for her hypertension and metformin 500mg daily for her diabetes.
She also mentioned having white coat syndrome, with a history of high blood pressure readings in the office but normal readings at home.
She reported having difficulty adhering to a healthy diet, frequently consuming fast food and sweets.
She has a history of insomnia and has tried meditation apps and sleep hygiene practices to help with sleep.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes, and has been taking norvasc 2.5mg daily and metformin 500mg daily.
She has had multiple episodes of kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
Review of systems: The patient reported no chest pain, abdominal pain, or other symptoms.
Current medications:
Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 150/90 mmHg, no other vital signs mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's lungs were clear bilaterally, and no crackles were heard.
The heart exam revealed a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The abdominal exam was negative for tenderness to palpation or tenderness of the CVA.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:

Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, which are likely to continue until the underlying cause is addressed, as well as hypertension and diabetes, which require ongoing management.
Problem list: Kidney stones; Hypertension; Diabetes; Insomnia; Recurrent kidney stones; Hypertension; Diabetes; Insomnia; Suspected underlying cause of kidney stones.
Plan:
I prescribed percocet as needed for pain management.
I will give the patient a referral to urology to investigate the underlying cause of her recurrent kidney stones.
I will also order a urinalysis and urine culture to ensure that the current stone has passed and there are no remaining issues.
I will give the patient a consult to nutrition to help her with her diet and adherence to a healthy eating plan.
I will also prescribe ten milligrams of melatonin as needed for insomnia.
Follow-up: The patient will return in three months for a follow-up appointment.
